DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JULY 1, 2013 TO PERMIT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE TO FILE A NEW REPLY IN LIGHT OF DR. YUKHIMOVA'S NOTICE OF ERRATA SERVED ON JUNE 10, 2013 AND FILED ON JUNE 11, 2013. THE NEW REPLY SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED BY PERSONAL DELIVERY, FAX OR EMAIL NO LATER THAN JUNE 24, 2013 AT 12 PM. COURTESY COPIES ARE TO BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302 AT THE TIME OF FILING. ALSO, THE COURT IS NOT IN RECEIPT OF COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY FILED ON JUNE 7, 2013. COURTESY COPIES OF THE ENDORSED STAMPED REPLY FILED ON JUNE 7, 2013 ARE TO BE PROVIDED TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JUNE 24, 2013 AT 12 PM. LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S SIGNATURE TO AUTHORIZATIONS TO RELEASE SENSITIVE RECORDS AND INFORMATION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF PERSUASION THAT WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE 10850 DOES NOT PROTECT THE RECORDS COVERED BY THE SUBPOENA AND THAT WELFARE AND INSTITUTIONS CODE 10850.2 AUTHORIZES THIS COURT TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO AUTHORIZE DEFENDANT OR ITS COUNSEL TO VIEW THE RECORDS COVERED BY THE SUBPOENA FOR THE PURPOSES SOUGHT HERE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: JO BREWER, CSR # 9707
ORDER DENYING GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S SIGNATURE TO AUTHORIZATIONS TO RELEASE SENSITIVE RECORDS AND INFORMATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION'S IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON THE INDIVIDUAL CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT IS LIABLE FOR MR. SCAMIN'S CONDUCT BECAUSE DEFENDANT OWED A NON-DELEGABLE DUTY TO PLAINTIFF BASED UPON 22 CCR 54309(A)(8) AND DEFENDANT'S CONTRACT WITH CALIFORNIA. "IF'AN INDIVIDUAL OR CORPORATION UNDERTAKES TO CARRY ON AN ACTIVITY INVOLVING POSSIBLE DANGER TO THE PUBLIC UNDER A LICENSE OR FRANCHISE GRANTED BY PUBLIC AUTHORITY SUBJECT TO CERTAIN OBLIGATIONS OR LIABILITIES IMPOSED BY THE PUBLIC AUTHORITY, THESE LIABILITIES MAY NOT BE EVADED BY DELEGATING PERFORMANCE TO AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE ORIGINAL CONTRACTOR REMAINS SUBJECT TO LIABILITY FOR HARM CAUSED BY THE NEGLIGENCE OF THE INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR EMPLOYED TO DO THE WORK." TAYLOR V. OAKLAND SCAVENGER CO. (1941) 17 CAL.2D AT 594, 604. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH A CONSCIOUS DISREGARD AS TO PLAINTIFF'S SAFETY. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN WITH REGARD TO WHETHER A CORPORATE OFFICER, DIRECTOR, OR MANAGING AGENT ENGAGED IN THE MALICIOUS CONDUCT. THAT ISSUE WAS FIRST RAISED IN THE REPLY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT OLEG SCAMIN, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA SCOL, CO'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED, IN PART, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. PLAINTIFF'S TREATING PHYSICIAN PUT HER NEUROLOGICAL DEFICITS AT ISSUE IN HIS DEPOSITION. THE ENTIRE EXAMINATION SHALL NOT LAST LONGER THAN 2 HOURS AND WILL BE CONDUCTED BY PETER C. CASSINI, M.D., A LICENSED NEUROLOGIST AT PLAINTIFF'S RESIDENCE OR A MUTUALLY AGREED UPON LOCATION BY THE PARTIES. THE EXAMINATION WILL INCLUDE TAKING A MEDICALLY RELEVANT HISTORY. THE EXAMINATION WILL INCLUDE A GENERAL PHYSICAL NEUROLOGIC EXAM OF CRANIAL NERVES, MOTOR FUNCTION, DEXTERITY AND SENSORY FUNCTION. THESE TESTS ARE TO BE DONE IN A PAINLESS, PASSIVE MANNER. DEPENDING ON WHAT IS REVEALED IN THE GENERAL EXAMINATION, A MORE FOCUSED, DETAILED PHYSICAL EXAMINATION MAY BE CONDUCTED TO BETTER IDENTIFY THE SOURCE OF ANY SPECIFIC PROBLEMS. THE EXAMINATION IS NOT TO INVOLVE ANY PAINFUL OR PROTRACTED TESTING. NO X-RAYS OR ANY ELECTRONIC OR MAGNETIC SCANNING WILL BE PERFORMED DURING THE EXAMINATION. PLAINTIFF SHALL COOPERATE DURING THE EXAMINATION SO THAT IT MAY BE COMPLETED WITHIN THE 2 HOUR LIMITATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: L. FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: ATTY SANFORD M. CIPINKO, LAW OFFICES OF SANFORD M. CIPINKO, 415.693.9905, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF YUKIMOVA;
ATTY CHRISTOPHER F. JOHNSON, MARANGA MORENSTERN, 818.587.9146, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE;
ATTY NATHAN L. WINEGAR, CODDINGTON HICKS & DANFORTH, 650.592.5400, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-DEEFENDANT OLEG SCAMIN BY COURT CALL.

Ruling A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION'S is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to shift the burden on the individual causes of action. Defendant is liable for Mr. Scamin's conduct because defendant owed a non-delegable duty to plaintiff based upon 22 CCR 54309(a)(8) and defendant's contract with California. "If'an individual or corporation undertakes to carry on an activity involving possible danger to the public under a license or franchise granted by public authority subject to certain obligations or liabilities imposed by the public authority, these liabilities may not be evaded by delegating performance to an independent contractor. The original contractor remains subject to liability for harm caused by the negligence of the independent contractor employed to do the work." Taylor v. Oakland Scavenger Co. (1941) 17 Cal.2d at 594, 604. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant acted with a conscious disregard as to plaintiff's safety. Defendant fails to shift the burden with regard to whether a corporate officer, director, or managing agent engaged in the malicious conduct. That issue was first raised in the reply. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC's Motion For An Order To Compel Plaintiff's Signature To Authorizations To Release Sensitive Records And Information is Denied. Moving party has failed to meet its burden of persuasion that Welfare and Institutions Code 10850 does not protect the records covered by the subpoena and that Welfare and Institutions Code 10850.2 authorizes this Court to compel Plaintiff to authorize Defendant or its counsel to view the records covered by the subpoena for the purposes sought here. Defendant Oleg Scamin, Individually And Dba Scol, Co'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Jun-14-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GAIL J. LAMCHICK, ESQ., 650.851.8880, FOR PLAINTIFF MARIYA YUKHIMOVA.
NATHAN WINEGAR, ESQ., 650.592.5400, FOR DEFENDNAT OLEG SCAMIN.
CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, ESQ., 415.248.5315, FOR DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Oleg Scamin, Individually And Dba Scol, Co'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination IS Granted, in part, for good cause shown. Plaintiff's treating physician put her neurological deficits at issue in his deposition. The entire examination shall not last longer than 2 hours and will be conducted by Peter C. Cassini, M.D., a licensed neurologist at Plaintiff's residence or a mutually agreed upon location by the parties. The examination will include taking a medically relevant history. The examination will include a general physical neurologic exam of cranial nerves, motor function, dexterity and sensory function. These tests are to be done in a painless, passive manner. Depending on what is revealed in the general examination, a more focused, detailed physical examination may be conducted to better identify the source of any specific problems. The examination is not to involve any painful or protracted testing. No x-rays or any electronic or magnetic scanning will be performed during the examination. Plaintiff shall cooperate during the examination so that it may be completed within the 2 hour limitation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: L. Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-14-2013 Continued To Jul-01-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GAIL J. LAMCHICK, ESQ., 650.851.8880, FOR PLAINTIFF MARIYA YUKHIMOVA.
NATHAN WINEGAR, ESQ., 650.592.5400, FOR DEFENDNAT OLEG SCAMIN.
CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, ESQ., 415.248.5315, FOR DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS Continued on the court's own motion to July 1, 2013 to permit Golden State Adult Day Health Care to file a new reply in light of Dr. Yukhimova's Notice of Errata served on June 10, 2013 and filed on June 11, 2013. The new reply shall be filed and served by personal delivery, fax or email no later than June 24, 2013 at 12 pm. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 at the time of filing. Also, the Court is not in receipt of courtesy copies of the reply filed on June 7, 2013.
